No price = unavailable: admin grid yellow + V2 sync is_available fix

1. Admin grid: dates with no cm_price/direct_price now show yellow
   (blocked) instead of green, matching the unbookable state.

2. V2 Calendar sync: when Beds24 returns numAvail > 0 but no price,
   set is_available = false. A room without pricing cannot be booked.
   Applied to all 8 Beds24 V2 Calendar write locations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

V1 fallback: fix skip logic — check date coverage not just priced rows

Rooms with a few V2 rows (all priced) were skipped because all_priced
check only compared existing rows. Villa Loupa had 5 priced rows but
rate rules cover 210 dates. Now skip only when existing priced rows
outnumber rate rule dates (PriceLabs rooms with full coverage).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

V1 fallback: INSERT rows for all dates covered by rate rules

Previously the fallback had separate UPDATE (for existing rows) and
INSERT (for zero-row rooms) paths. Rooms like Villa Loupa had a few V2
rows with prices, so they hit the UPDATE path which only touched NULL
prices — missing dates were never created. Now uses a single unified
path: INSERT ON CONFLICT for every date in rate rules, preserving
existing V2/PriceLabs prices with COALESCE.

confluence-mdx: 문장 분리 시 닫는 괄호 뒤에서도 분리되도록 개선합니다 (#882)

## Summary
- `split_into_sentences()` 정규식에서 마침표/느낌표/물음표 뒤 닫는 괄호 `)`도 문장 종결로 인식하도록
개선합니다.
- 예: `입니다.) 따라서` → `입니다.)` 와 `따라서` 로 분리
- `test_split_into_sentences.py` 테스트를 추가합니다.

## Test plan
- [x] `pytest tests/test_split_into_sentences.py` 통과
- [x] `pytest tests/` 전체 통과 (775 passed, 기존 실패 2건은 로컬 데이터 의존)
- [x] `npm test` 전체 통과 (74 tests)

commit.message:
Fix connection pool exhaustion: consolidate duplicate pools into one

Two separate pg.Pool instances (db/index.ts and server/db.ts) each with
max 20 connections were exhausting DigitalOcean's ~22 connection limit.
server/db.ts now re-exports from the shared pool. Max reduced to 10.
Also removed unused DATABASE_URL_OVERRIDE and added graceful exit to
drizzle.config.ts when DATABASE_URL is not set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add --no-renames to git log to prevent misordered migrations (#18)

Without --no-renames, git's rename detection can cause a renamed
migration file (e.g. when changing its revision ID) to be classified
as a rename rather than an add. --diff-filter=A then silently excludes
the file, which causes it to receive the fallback uncommitted_seq
ordering — placing it at the tail of the chain regardless of its
actual position in git history.

This led to a production incident where a migration was inserted
before an already-applied migration, causing alembic to silently skip
it (ghost predecessor).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- 03_Analysis/company_dives/ (per-company forensic scripts)

Files remain local (gitignored). References updated in README and CLAUDE.md.

feat: Code-Suche liest jetzt Dateien + Modell-Aufteilung

## Code-Suche Fix
- search_code liest jetzt automatisch die gefundenen Dateien
- read_file kann relative Pfade aus Java/Python Repos auflösen
- Dateiinhalt wird direkt zurückgegeben (max 10KB pro Datei)

## Modell-Aufteilung
- Neues Setting: llm.tool_model (schnelles Modell für Suche)
- Neues Setting: llm.analysis_model (großes Modell für Analyse)
- Tool-Phase: Verwendet tool_model für schnelle Dateisuche
- Analyse-Phase: Verwendet analysis_model für finale Antwort

Beispiel config.yaml:
  llm:
    tool_model: "gptoss120b"      # Schnell für Suche
    analysis_model: "qwen-428b"   # Groß für Analyse

feat: Module 16 — Docker deployment for zero-config local sharing

- Dockerfile: python:3.11-slim + Ollama install + pip deps + project copy
- docker-compose.yml: bind-mounts for data/uploads, exports, knowledge (data stays on host); named volume for Ollama model cache (9GB downloaded once); 10GB memory limit
- entrypoint.sh: starts ollama serve, waits with 30s timeout, idempotent model pulls (llama3.1:8b + llava), then launches Streamlit on 0.0.0.0:8501
- .dockerignore: excludes user data dirs, cache, exports, tests, .env, .git, binaries
- Makefile: 13 targets — start, start-bg, stop, restart, logs, build, reset, update, shell, check, switch-small, network, status
- check_requirements.sh: pre-flight checks for Docker, RAM (≥8GB), disk (≥10GB), port availability, project files
- README_SETUP.md: plain-English setup guide for non-technical users (Mac + Windows), Steps 1-6 + Troubleshooting
- Data privacy: all user data volumes are host bind-mounts — nothing transmitted externally

Verification: 10/10 file checks pass; pre-flight script: 10/13 (3 Docker checks expected to fail without Docker Desktop)

commit.message:
fix: deploy built output instead of source directory to SWA

The SWA was serving the raw index.html (referencing /src/main.tsx) instead
of the Vite-built output, causing a white screen. With skip_app_build: true,
output_location is not relative to app_location — changed app_location to
web/dist directly. Moved staticwebapp.config.json into public/ so Vite
copies it to dist during build.

commit.message:
fix(backend): prevent DetachedInstanceError in GitHub write-back tasks (v1.1.1)

After db.commit(), SQLAlchemy expires all ORM attributes by default
(expire_on_commit=True). Background tasks run after db.close(), so
accessing paper/db_analysis attributes causes DetachedInstanceError,
silently killing the GitHub write-back.

Fix: call db.refresh() on both objects after commit but before close,
forcing attribute reload into memory while the session is still open.

Affects: /api/mcp/analyze and /api/mcp/analyze-deep in webmcp.py
The /analyze endpoint in main.py was already correct (had db.refresh).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
